title: selaginella luzonensis
intro: Selaginella luzonensis, also known as the "living fossil" due to its unique, ancient appearance, is a species of plant within the Selaginellaceae family. It is native to the Philippines and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and potentially aiding in the treatment of certain diseases. Herbal preparations of Selaginella luzonensis can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its safety and efficacy as a medicinal treatment.
